section.title: Overview
section.para: This module contains rdn (railway dedicated network) related functions for an IMS environment (S-CSCF and/or I-CSCF).

This module is most useful for terminating unregistered services that are triggered by the I/S-CSCF and that are identified by hierarchical address ranges of E.164 addresses and of EIRENE addresses (in the Request-URI), not unlike wildcarded PSIs.
By configuring these address ranges at the I/S-CSCF, one can save one SAR/SAA exchange with the HSS per call.
EIRENE addresses are railway specific telephone numbers, which have been defined by the UIC. This explains the name of the module RDN = Railway Dedicated Networks.

Additionally, this module defines kamailio transformations, which are tailored for the use in RDNs, e.g. for the transformation of functional numbers and of MCData SDS into instant messages and vice versa.
This module is even more useful, when implementing an IWF according to ETSI TS 103 792, in which case some specific routing scripts will be needed.
Following high level functions are provided by this module:

listitem.para: Definition of RDN Services with or without MCx AS Delivery
rdn_services.list: EIRENE RDN Services
rdn_services.list: E.164 RDN Services
dpsi.list: Distinct PSI RDN Services
dpsi.list: Domain Based RDN Services
analyse_eirene_ruri() - search EIRENE RDN Service from $ru
analyse_e164_ruri() - search E.164 RDN Service from $ru
analyse_dpsi_ruri() - search Distinct PSI RDN Service from $ru
analyse_domain_ruri() - search Domain based PSI RDN Service from $ru
prepare_term_unreg(domain) - prepare service, after it has been found by analyse_..._ruri()
analyse_eirene_pvar("pv") - search EIRENE RDN Service from PV
analyse_e164_ruri() - search E.164 RDN Service from PV
analyse_dpsi_ruri() - search Distinct PSI RDN Service from PV
analyse_domain_ruri() - search Domain based PSI RDN Service from PV

section.title: &kamailio; Modules
section.para: After kamailio core modules the following modules must be loaded before this module:

listitem.para: TM - Transaction Manager
ims_userloc_scscf (if used on S-CSCF)
ims_registrar_scscf (if used on S-CSCF)

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para: This module requires the internal IMS library.

section.title: list_file (e.g. rdn_services.list)
section.para: This file contains the digit combinations with their respective application server/service key.

example.title: list_file parameter usage
example.programlisting:
... modparam("ims_rdn", "list_file", "/usr/etc/kamailio-scscf-ref/rdn_services.list") ...
rdn_services.list entry example: ... # digits application server sessiontype hardcoded-RURI DEL INSRT 0432 sip:my-as.com first-answer sip:srvc@ims.net 3 43 +4350255 sip:my-as.com private sip:srvc@ims.net 0 +49 sip:my-as.com private ...
NOTE: lets assume a Req-URI sip:043287654321@ims.net;user=gsmr is received. In this case the first row will apply, where hardcoded-RURI, DEL and INSRT are not empty.
I.e. - we have an EIRENE RDN Service WITH MCx AS Delivery.
Lets assume a Req-URI sip:+43502553468@ims.net;user=phone is received. In this case the second row will apply and we have an E.164 RDN Service WITH MCx AS Delivery.
Lets assume a Req-URI sip:+49684512345@ims.net;user=phone is received. In this case the third row will apply and we have an E.164 RDN Service withOUT MCx AS Delivery (4th, 5th and 6th column are empty). The Request will not receive a "special MCPTT handling" by the routing configuration
NOTE: The digits field must contain digits only - kamailio server fails to start in case digits field contains non digit characters.

section.title: dpsi_file (e.g. dpsi.list)
section.para: This file contains the distinct public service identifiers with their respective application server/sessiontype, and it contains the descriptions of the domain based RDN services.

example.title: dpsi_file parameter usage
example.programlisting:
... modparam("ims_rdn", "dpsi_file", "/usr/etc/kamailio-scscf-ref/dpsi.list") ...
dpsi.list entry example: ... # service application server sessiontype service34 sip:172.29.21.20:5060 mcptt34 MESSAGE@ipsmgw.com sip:172.29.21.19:5060 ipsmgw ...
NOTE: Size of hash table is currently optimized for 64 entries.

section.title: cx_userdata_templates (e.g. CxUserData_tmpl.xml)
section.para: This file is a part of the common configuration and should only be changed, if you really know what you do.

section.title: rdn_node_type (integer)
section.para: This parameter replaces the old i_am_at_icscf flag and defines the node type where the ims_rdn module is running.

Depending on the value, the module will behave accordingly, especially in terms of database interactions, and whether certain functions such as prepare_fa_delivery() and prepare_term_unreg() are available, which are only applicable when operating as an S-CSCF.

When using Multi-MCx Domain Support (i.e., when rdn_node_type is set to 16 or 17), two additional columns are expected for each entry in rdn_services.list and dpsi.list: MCx Domain and IWF Domain. These must be inserted after the AS/S-CSCF FQDN and after the session type. If the support is not enabled (i.e., rdn_node_type < 16), these columns must not be present.

Note: The optional S-CSCF flag is treated as part of the AS/S-CSCF FQDN field and is not counted as a separate column. Therefore, the FQDN is considered to be in column 3, and the session type in column 4 (unless MCx support or special handling shifts this further).

listitem.para: MCx domain indicates the source domain (e.g., for the originating UE)
IWF domain defines the target domain (e.g., for the terminating UE or IWF AS)
Hardcoded R-URI defines the target AS or gateway for MCx delivery

section.para: If any of these fields are missing, the module will reject the configuration entry at startup and log an error such as: Cannot add RDN service. Special handling needs hardcoded RURI(...)
Supported values:

listitem.para: 0 S-CSCF (default mode)
1 I-CSCF
16 S-CSCF with Multi MCx Domain Support
17 I-CSCF with Multi MCx Domain Support

section.para: The function presumes that the user has checked, whether the Req-URI actually contains an EIRENE FN (e.g. by checking for the URI parameter "user=gsmr").
The function searches for a contiguous block of digits in the userpart of the Req-URI and compares these digits with the stored digit tree of EIRENE RDN Services.
If it finds a matching EIRENE RDN Service in the file rdn_services.list, then it stores the values from the found row in AVPs and returns positive.
If it encounters a problem, e.g. if no service is found, then a negative result is returned.
Negative return codes can be interpreted as follows:

listitem.para: -1 (generic error, service not found) - a generic error has happened or the service has not been found (e.g. Req-URI cannot be parsed, no matching service found for this Req-URI)
-2 (AVPs could not be stored) - everything went well, but at last the AVPs could not be stored, PVs will be incorrect

section.para: Positive return codes can be interpreted as follows:

listitem.para: 1 (OK) - everything went well. The next action should be calling prepare_term_unreg() to prepare the DB for the ims_isc module

2 (Mx-Delivery) - everything went well. However the hardcoded-RURI (4th column in the file rdn_services.list) does not contain a userpart, i.e. it is an FQDN (of the AS), but not a PSI. Hence the IMS Subscription cannot be stored to DB and calling prepare_term_unreg() will be useless. The routing script should deliver the Request to the AS via the Mx interface ("per FQDN", but not "per PSI")

section.para: This function can be used in REQUEST_ROUTE only.

example.title: analyse_eirene_ruri usage

section.para: Same function like analyse_eirene_ruri(), just it applies to E.164 RDN Services in the file rdn_services.list.
This function can be used in REQUEST_ROUTE only.

example.title: analyse_e164_ruri usage
example.programlisting: ... see the example at function analyse_eirene_ruri ...

section.para: Same function like analyse_eirene_ruri(), just it applies to DPSIs in the file dpsi.list.
This function can be used in REQUEST_ROUTE only.

example.title: analyse_dpsi_ruri usage
example.programlisting: ... see the example at function analyse_eirene_ruri ...

section.para: Same function like analyse_eirene_ruri(), just it applies to domain based RDN Services in the file dpsi.list.
This function can be used in REQUEST_ROUTE only.

example.title: analyse_domain_ruri usage
example.programlisting: ... see the example at function analyse_eirene_ruri ...

para.emphasis: This function is not applicable at an I-CSCF

section.para: The function presumes that the user has called one of the functions analyse_eirene_ruri(), analyse_e164_ruri(), analyse_dpsi_ruri() or analyse_domain_ruri() and the result was 1.

It looks up the stored values in the AVPs and tries to update the scscf database with an unregistered subscription. IMS subscription data are prepared including application server name for routing the INVITE/ PUBLISH/ SUBSCRIBE/ MESSAGE/ or REFER/ message to the Application Server. The information about the service is kept in the AVPs and can still be read out from the routing configuration by using the PVs defined by this module.
If it encounters a problem, e.g. if the DB cannot be accessed, then a negative result is returned.
Negative return codes can be interpreted as follows:

listitem.para: -1 (generic error) - a generic error has happened (e.g. Cx user data could not be parsed, contacts could not be updated in DB)

section.para: Positive return codes can be interpreted as follows::

listitem.para: 1 (OK) - everything went well. The next call of isc_match_filter("term","location") - see the description of module ims_isc - will deliver the request to the AS

section.para: Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: domain - Logical domain within registrar.

section.para: This function can be used in REQUEST_ROUTE only.

example.title: prepare_term_unreg usage
example.programlisting: ... see the example at function analyse_eirene_ruri ...

section.para: Returns the value of the 2nd column of the found row of tables rdn_services.list or dpsi.list, after one of the functions prepare_fa_delivery(), analyse_eirene_ruri(), analyse_e164_ruri(), analyse_dpsi_ruri() or analyse_domain_ruri() has successfully detected an RDN Service or a DPSI.
This PV is used on the S-CSCF. Please refer to rdn_scscf_name for I-CSCF.

Returns the value of the 2nd column of the found row of tables rdn_services.list or dpsi.list, after one of the functions prepare_fa_delivery(), analyse_eirene_ruri(), analyse_e164_ruri(), analyse_dpsi_ruri() or analyse_domain_ruri() has successfully detected an RDN Service or a DPSI.
This PV is used on the I-CSCF. Please refer to rdn_as_name for S-CSCF.

Returns the value of the 3rd column of the found row of tables rdn_services.list or dpsi.list, after one of the functions prepare_fa_delivery(), analyse_eirene_ruri(), analyse_e164_ruri(), analyse_dpsi_ruri() or analyse_domain_ruri() has successfully detected an RDN Service or a DPSI.

Returns the value of the 4th column of the found row of tables rdn_services.list, if it exists, after one of the functions prepare_fa_delivery(), analyse_eirene_ruri() or analyse_e164_ruri() has successfully detected an RDN Service.
If this PV is $null or an empty string, then the 4th, 5th and 6th column of the found row are empty. Hence we have an "RDN Service WITHOUT MCx AS Delivery (no MCPTT handling necessary)
If this PV is a string with the value "*", then the routing configuration SHALL NOT replace the Req-URI with the value of this PV.
Otherwise, the routing configuration shall replace the Req-URI with this value, before calling isc_match_filter().

example.title: rdn_hardcoded_ruri pseudo-variable usage
example.programlisting: ... ## flexible modification of Request URI (depends on position in digit tree) ## the PVs $rdn_sessiontype, $rdn_hardcoded_ruri, $rdn_insrt and $rdn_del ## have been looked up by the function prepare_fa_delivery and can be ## different for each RDN service ## make an E.164 from a CT8 ## make a functional alias from a CT5 ## make a functional alias from a CT1 ## make a functional alias from a CT7 if ($rdn_sessiontype == "prearranged") $var(ibsipr) = "sip:g.mcptt." + $rdn_insrt + $(rU{s.substr,$rdn_del,0}) + ".prearranged@" + NETWORKNAME + ";user=gsmr"; else $var(ibsipr) = "sip:+" + $rdn_insrt + $(rU{s.substr,$rdn_del,0}) + "@" + NETWORKNAME + ";user=phone"; $ru = $rdn_hardcoded_ruri; ...

section.para: Returns the value of the 5th column of the found row of the table rdn_services.list, after one of the functions prepare_fa_delivery(), analyse_eirene_ruri() or analyse_e164_ruri() has successfully detected an RDN Service.

Returns the value of the 6th column of the found row of the table rdn_services.list, after one of the functions prepare_fa_delivery(), analyse_eirene_ruri() or analyse_e164_ruri() has successfully detected an RDN Service.
